payload.answer: Recall returned mem_new because it is the newer memory for Sam from Goldman, and mem_old was filtered out because the system marked it as superseded. In other words, the older fact was not treated as current.
payload.decision: Recall preferred mem_new because it is the newer, unsuperseded memory for the same entity context, while mem_old was treated as outdated and therefore excluded from the current result set.

entity_resolution.reason: The recall appears to refer to the Sam associated with Goldman; exact internal entity id is not provided, so the match is described at the name/context level only.

payload.intent: Explain the recall result for Sam from Goldman, specifically why mem_new was returned and mem_old was filtered as superseded.

memory_cards.statement: mem_new is the current memory to use for Sam from Goldman; it supersedes the earlier mem_old entry.
mem_old exists for the same Sam-from-Goldman context but is no longer current because it has been superseded.

repair_options: Keep mem_new as the active memory and retain mem_old only as historical/superseded context.
If the intended entity differs, add disambiguating context for which Sam or which Goldman reference should be recalled.
